steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 1: Barad's theory rebuts the subject-object divide that has defined scientific epistemology. Sentence 2: the theory represents a paradigmatic shift. The transition must express that sentence 2 follows as a consequence of sentence 1 — not an example, contradiction, or caveat.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: Introduces exemplification — sentence 2 is not an example of sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice B — By contrast,: Signals contradiction — sentence 2 reinforces and extends sentence 1, not opposes it. ✗
  • Choice C — As such,: Means "therefore" or "in this manner" — it connects the rebuke (sentence 1) to its logical consequence: a paradigmatic shift (sentence 2). ✓
  • Choice D — That said,: Introduces a concession or qualification, but sentence 2 amplifies rather than qualifies sentence 1. ✗
That matches Choice C — As such,.
